title: Bitter Crumbly Russian Tuna Salad
description: A refreshing lunch featuring raw vegetables, crumbled feta, and a tangy deglazed vinaigrette that gives the dish a delightful bitter bite.

ingredients.name: Canned tuna in olive oil
ingredients.notes: Provides protein and a subtle brine flavor

instructions: 1. In a small saucepan, heat 1 tsp of white wine vinegar over medium heat until it reduces slightly, creating a deglazing liquid; set aside.
2. In a large bowl, combine the diced cucumber, carrot, beet, and shredded red cabbage.
3. Add the drained tuna to the vegetable mixture, breaking it up gently with a fork.
4. Sprinkle the crumbled feta cheese and chopped dill over the salad.
5. In a separate small bowl, whisk together the lemon juice, olive oil, Dijon mustard, reduced vinegar, salt, and pepper to make a vinaigrette.
6. Pour the vinaigrette over the salad and toss gently to coat all ingredients evenly.
7. Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary, adding more salt, pepper, or lemon juice to enhance the bitterness and brightness.
8. Let the salad rest for 5 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
9. Serve chilled or at room temperature, garnished with a few extra dill sprigs if desired.
